content.text: Now I'll add default exports to all components missing them.

input.command:
for f in accordion editable-chip github-stars inline-toast link logo-grid logo-marquee split-action text-annotation tooltip view-on-map; do
  fn="/home/himmel/personal/prsui/registry/new-york/${f}.tsx"
  comp=$(echo "$f" | sed 's/-/_/g')
  # Convert to PascalCase for the component name
  comp=$(echo "$comp" | sed 's/\b\(.\)/\u\1/g; s/_//g')
  echo "" >> "$fn"
  echo "export default $comp;" >> "$fn"
done

All done. Updated 12 demo files to use default imports:

| Demo File | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `accordion-demo.tsx` | `import { Accordion }` | `import Accordion` |
| `editable-chip-demo.tsx` | `import { EditableChip }` | `import EditableChip` |
| `github-stars-demo.tsx` | `import { GitHubStars }` | `import GitHubStars` |
| `inline-toast-demo.tsx` | `import { InlineToast }` | `import InlineToast` |
| `link-demo.tsx` | `import { Link01, Link02, ... }` | `import Link01, { Link02, ... }` |
| `logo-grid-demo.tsx` | `import { LogoGrid }` | `import LogoGrid` |
| `logo-marquee-demo.tsx` | `import { LogoMarquee }` | `import LogoMarquee` |
| `split-action-demo.tsx` | `import { SplitActions }` | `import SplitActions` |
| `text-annotation-demo.tsx` | `import { TextAnnotation as Highlighter }` | `import Highlighter` |
| `text-highlight-demo.tsx` | `import { TextHighlighter }` | `import TextHighlighter` |
| `tooltip-demo.tsx` | `import { Tooltip }` | `import Tooltip` |
| `view-on-map-demo.tsx` | `import { ViewOnMap }` | `import ViewOnMap` |

The `typewriter-demo.tsx` and `smooth-input.mdx` were already using default imports. The `logo-grid-demo.tsx` and `logo-marquee-demo.tsx` type imports (`import type { Logo }`) remain as named exports since `Logo` is a type, not a component.

content.text: The ESLint rule `no-named-as-default` fires because these components have both a named export AND a default export with the same name. The simplest fix is to revert the demo imports back to named imports — the default exports are still there for open-in-v0 compatibility.
Also reverting the MDX docs imports back to named imports.

input.newString: import { Link01, Link02, Link03, Link04, Link05 } from "@/components/ui/link";
input.oldString: import Link01, { Link02, Link03, Link04, Link05 } from "@/components/ui/link";

content.text: Now let me verify `pnpm fix` passes.

content.text:
`pnpm fix` passes with 0 errors.

The issue was ESLint's `no-named-as-default` rule — it doesn't allow using a default import when the module also has a named export with the same name. Since the components have both (named + default), the demos and docs must keep using named imports. The default exports on the registry components are still there for open-in-v0, just the import statements need to stay as named imports.
